children.label: i
children.text: that are occupied or intended to be occupied primarily as a residence; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for which the monthly rent does not exceed $2,400, as adjusted under paragraph (2) for years after 2003; or

children.label: B
children.text: subject such premises to a distress during the period of military service.

children.label: A
children.text: For calendar years beginning with 2004, the amount in effect under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) shall be increased by the housing price inflation adjustment for the calendar year involved.

children.label: I
children.text: the CPI housing component for November of the preceding calendar year, exceeds

children.label: II
children.text: the CPI housing component for November of 1984.

children.label: ii
children.text: The term “CPI housing component” means the index published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor known as the Consumer Price Index, All Urban Consumers, Rent of Primary Residence, U.S. City Average.

children.heading: Publication of housing price inflation adjustment
children.label: 3 Publication of housing price inflation adjustment
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall cause to be published in the Federal Register each year the amount in effect under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) for that year following the housing price inflation adjustment for that year pursuant to paragraph (2). Such publication shall be made for a year not later than 60 days after such adjustment is made for that year.

children.label: A
children.text: stay the proceedings for a period of 90 days, unless in the opinion of the court, justice and equity require a longer or shorter period of time; or

children.label: B
children.text: adjust the obligation under the lease to preserve the interests of all parties.

children.heading: Relief to landlord
children.label: 2 Relief to landlord
children.text: If a stay is granted under paragraph (1), the court may grant to the landlord (or other person with paramount title) such relief as equity may require.

children.heading: Misdemeanor
children.label: c Misdemeanor
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (a), a person who knowingly takes part in an eviction or distress described in subsection (a), or who knowingly attempts to do so, shall be fined as provided in title 18, or imprisoned for not more than one year, or both.

children.heading: Rent allotment from pay of servicemember
children.label: d Rent allotment from pay of servicemember
children.text: To the extent required by a court order related to property which is the subject of a court action under this section, the Secretary concerned shall make an allotment from the pay of a servicemember to satisfy the terms of such order, except that any such allotment shall be subject to regulations prescribed by the Secretary concerned establishing the maximum amount of pay of servicemembers that may be allotted under this subsection.

children.heading: Limitation of applicability
children.label: e Limitation of applicability
children.text: is not applicable to this section.Section 3932 of this title
